messages.content: Build a pure Decider for a Warehouse aggregate in TypeScript. Support open, expand, and decommission commands. Throw a typed DomainError when decommissioning a warehouse that still has active bins.
Write a Bin Decider in TypeScript with allocate, restock, and retire commands. Strict types, discriminated unions, exhaustive never defaults. A retired bin cannot be restocked.
Explain briefly how a Lot Decider enforces invariants, then implement one in TypeScript with create, quarantine, and release transitions. A quarantined lot must be released before any consumption.

Implement a Serial (serialised unit tracker) Decider in TypeScript. Commands: issue, attach-to-crate, scrap. A serial that has been scrapped can never be attached. Use discriminated unions and strict types.
Build a Packing station Decider in TypeScript. Commands: start, scan-item, seal. Cannot seal an empty pack. Use a discriminated union for state and throw a typed PackingError on invariants.
What's the v5 way to model a Crate Decider in TypeScript that enforces load → seal → dispatch transitions? Show a minimal typed example.
Write a TypeScript projection that folds Warehouse events into a query-friendly occupancy view. Use a pure project(view, event) function, ignore irrelevant events, and keep the switch exhaustive.
Write a TypeScript projection for Bin inventory. Maintain a per-sku total across all bins. Use project(view, event), ignore events that don't touch quantities, and exhaustive matching.
Add a denormalised count-by-status projection to this event type. Use project(view, event) in TypeScript and keep the pure-function shape.
Implement a SkuMovement projection in TypeScript. It folds inbound/outbound events into a per-sku ledger with opening balance, in, out, and on-hand. Use project(view, event).

Implement snapshot + versioned upgrade for a Container aggregate in TypeScript. Include CURRENT_VERSION, two upgraders v1->v2 and v2->v3, a loader that runs them in order, and a final type assertion that the result matches CurrentState.

Explain how Snapshot versioning lets an event-sourced PickList evolve its schema without replaying everything, then implement one in TypeScript with CURRENT_VERSION, two upgraders, and an `upgradeSnapshot` loader.
Write a snapshot loader for a Warehouse aggregate in TypeScript. Include CURRENT_VERSION=4, three ordered upgraders, and fail loudly on an unknown future version.

Implement optimistic concurrency for a Bin stream in TypeScript using Effect.gen for the IO boundary. Define a local EventStore interface, read the current revision, append with expectedRevision, and raise a typed ConcurrencyConflict on mismatch.
How do I guard an Outbound stream write with expectedRevision in TypeScript? Show a minimal example with a local EventStore interface and a typed ConcurrencyConflict.

Add optimistic concurrency to a ReceivingDock stream in TypeScript. Show the read + compute + append loop with expectedRevision, a single retry, and a typed ConcurrencyConflict when the second attempt also fails.
Build a CommandHandler in TypeScript that orchestrates a Lot aggregate over a local EventStore. Load the stream, decide, append with expectedRevision. No business logic in the handler.
Write a CommandHandler in TypeScript for a Packing aggregate using Effect.gen to fence the EventStore boundary. Keep decide pure and strict-typed.
Build a subscription runner in TypeScript that projects Bin restock events into a read model and persists a checkpoint after each event. Define CheckpointStore locally and make writes crash-safe.
How do I resume a Packing projection from its last checkpoint after a restart? Show a minimal TypeScript example with a local CheckpointStore interface and a crash-safe apply-then-checkpoint loop.

Add checkpoint-resume behaviour to a SkuMovement subscription in TypeScript wrapped with Effect.gen at the IO boundary. Constraints: strict types, no any, local CheckpointStore, apply-before-checkpoint ordering.
Write a generic aggregateStream helper in TypeScript that folds an AsyncIterable of events into an aggregate state using evolve. Then use it for a Warehouse aggregate.
Implement a when-style reducer for a Lot aggregate in TypeScript. Use an exhaustive switch on event.type with a never default and a pure when(state, event) function.
What's the v5 way to aggregate a Serial event stream into current state using aggregateStream? Show a minimal TypeScript example.
Fold a PickList stream into current state in TypeScript. Use a pure when(state, event) with discriminated union events and a typed aggregate helper.
Upcast legacy Crate events to the current schema in TypeScript. Write an upcast(raw) function that handles two old shapes and throws UnknownEventError on anything else.
Explain briefly how upcasting lets us evolve ReceivingDock events without rewriting history, then write a TypeScript upcast function that migrates two legacy shapes to the current union.

Write a TypeScript read-model rebuild loop that re-projects a Bin inventory view from revision 0 in batches. Use a local EventStore iterator and a persist step per batch, with a resumable `from` option.
Add a resumable chunked rebuild to a SkuMovement read model in TypeScript. Constraints: strict types, `from` option, progress reporting callback, no inline any.

Build a minimal CQRS boundary for an Outbound aggregate in TypeScript. handleCommand writes events through decide + appendToStream; queryView reads from an eventually-consistent read store. No shared state type across the boundary.
Refactor this ad-hoc PickList code into a CQRS split in TypeScript: separate handleCommand (decide + appendToStream) from queryView (read model). No shared state type across the boundary.

Write a stateless process manager (saga) in TypeScript that reacts to Outbound events and emits Packing commands. Supports OutboundPrepared → StartPacking → PackingSealed → DispatchOutbound. Pure react(event) function.

Explain how a Lot/Serial saga keeps cross-aggregate workflows eventually consistent, then implement a pure react(event) in TypeScript that drives LotReleased → IssueSerial → SerialIssued → RecordInventory.

Translate Warehouse domain events into integration events for an outbox in TypeScript. Pure translate(event) function, versioned IntegrationEvent schema, and at least one domain event that maps to null.

How do I atomically append Container events and outbox rows in one transaction in TypeScript? Show the shape of `appendWithOutbox` with a local transactional interface; do not import pg. Include a comment describing the publisher's role.
Add an idempotent revision-aware append helper to a Serial stream in TypeScript. It should call appendToStream with expectedRevision, treat duplicate appends as no-ops, and return the final revision.
